title: RMS speed and average kinetic energy of gas molecules
description: Let's solve some problems to better understand how to find the rms speed and average KE of gas molecules.
topic_path: RMS speed and average kinetic energy of gas molecules

assessment_items.question_content:
Oxygen and Hydrogen are kept at the **same temperature**. 

**Choose the correct statements about the rms speeds of the gases and the kinetic energies of their molecules.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Gases of various molecular weights are kept at the **same temperature**.

**Choose the correct statements about the rms speeds of the gases and the kinetic energies of their molecules.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rms speed of the heaviest gas will be highest.
The rms speed of the heaviest gas will be lowest.
The rms speed of all gases will be the same.
The KE of the heaviest gas will be the highest.
The KE of the heaviest gas will be the lowest.
The KE of all gases will be the same.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does the rms speed of a gas change when its temperature doubles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The rms speed of a gas doubles.

**What change in temperature can cause this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The pressure of a gas is increased by decreasing its volume while keeping the temperature constant.

**Which of these best describes the cause of the increase in pressure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pressure increases because molecules have more speed.
Pressure increases because molecules strike the walls more often.
Pressure increases because molecules strike the walls with higher force.

assessment_items.question_content:
The pressure of a gas is decreased by increasing its volume while keeping the temperature constant.

**Which of these best describes the cause of the decrease in pressure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pressure decreases because molecules have less speed.
Pressure decreases because molecules strike the walls less often.
Pressure decreases because molecules strike the walls with lower force.

assessment_items.question_content:
**At what temperature is the rms speed of nitrogen the same as that of oxygen's rms speed at $0\degree\text C$?**  
*Note: Take $M_{\text O_2}=32\ \text u; M_{\text N_2}=28\ \text u$. Report your answer to two significant figures.*

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\degree\text C$
hints:
##Strategy

The rms speed of a gas is given by $v_{rms}=\sqrt{\dfrac{3\text RT}{M}}$

Equating the two rms speeds and using the molecular masses of oxygen and nitrogen, we can find the required temperature.

*Note: All temperatures are in Kelvin*

##Solution

$\begin{align}
v_{rms\text O_2} &= v_{rms\text N_2} \\\\
\Rightarrow \sqrt{\dfrac{T_{\text O_2}}{M_{\text O_2}}} &= \sqrt{\dfrac{T_{\text N_2}}{M_{\text N_2}}}  \\\\
\sqrt{\dfrac{273\ \text K}{32\ \text u}} &= \sqrt{\dfrac{T_{\text N_2}}{28\ \text u}}\\\\
\Rightarrow  T_{\text N_2}&\approx239\ \text K\\\\
&=-34\degree\text C
 \end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**At what temperature is the rms speed of hydrogen double that that of nitrogen's rms speed at $27\degree\text C$?**  
*Note: Take $M_{\text H_2}=2\ \text u; M_{\text N_2}=28\ \text u$. Report your answer to three significant figures.*

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\degree\text C$
hints:
##Strategy

The rms speed of a gas is given by $v_{rms}=\sqrt{\dfrac{3\text RT}{M}}$

Using the equation $v_{rms\text H_2} = 2v_{rms\text N_2}$ and using the molecular masses of oxygen and nitrogen, we can find the required temperature.

*Note: All temperatures are in Kelvin*

##Solution

$\begin{align}
v_{rms\text H_2} &= 2v_{rms\text N_2} \\\\
\Rightarrow \sqrt{\dfrac{T_{\text H_2}}{M_{\text H_2}}} &= 2\sqrt{\dfrac{T_{\text N_2}}{M_{\text N_2}}}  \\\\
\sqrt{\dfrac{T_{\text H_2}}{2\ \text u}} &= 2\sqrt{\dfrac{300\ \text K}{28\ \text u}}\\\\
\Rightarrow  T_{\text H_2}&\approx86\ \text K\\\\
&=-187\degree\text C
 \end{align}$
